title: Meridian Core Bond Fund
content:
MERIDIAN CORE BOND FUND - PRODUCT FACTSHEET

FUND OVERVIEW
The Meridian Core Bond Fund (Class A: MCBAX, Class I: MCBIX) is a diversified fixed income mutual fund designed to provide current income and preserve capital through professional management of high-quality bonds. The fund maintains a conservative investment approach focused on investment-grade corporate bonds, U.S. Treasury securities, and agency mortgage-backed securities.

INVESTMENT OBJECTIVE
The fund seeks to generate consistent income while maintaining principal stability through a diversified portfolio of fixed income securities. Our investment committee targets an average portfolio duration of 3.5-5.5 years to balance interest rate sensitivity with yield optimization.

PORTFOLIO COMPOSITION (as of March 2025)
• U.S. Treasury Securities: 35.2%
• Investment Grade Corporate Bonds: 42.8%
• Agency Mortgage-Backed Securities: 18.5%
• Cash and Cash Equivalents: 3.5%

Average Credit Quality: AA-
Average Duration: 4.7 years
Yield to Maturity: 4.85%
Number of Holdings: 247

PERFORMANCE DATA
As of March 31, 2025:
• YTD Return: 2.1%
• 1-Year Return: 5.8%
• 3-Year Annualized: 4.2%
• 5-Year Annualized: 3.9%
• Since Inception (06/15/2018): 4.1%

Benchmark: Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate Bond Index

FEES AND EXPENSES
Class A Shares:
• Gross Expense Ratio: 0.87%
• Net Expense Ratio: 0.75% (contractual fee waiver through 12/31/2025)
• Sales Load: 3.25% front-end
• Minimum Investment: $2,500

Class I Shares:
• Gross Expense Ratio: 0.62%
• Net Expense Ratio: 0.50%
• No Sales Load
• Minimum Investment: $250,000

RISK CONSIDERATIONS
While classified as conservative, investors should understand that bond funds carry interest rate risk, credit risk, and prepayment risk. Rising interest rates may cause bond prices to decline, affecting fund performance. The fund's focus on investment-grade securities helps mitigate credit risk, but does not eliminate the possibility of defaults or downgrades.

Duration risk remains the primary concern, with the fund's 4.7-year average duration indicating moderate sensitivity to interest rate changes. A 1% increase in interest rates could result in approximately 4.7% decline in fund value, all else being equal.

SUITABILITY ANALYSIS
In accordance with FINRA Rule 2111 suitability requirements, this fund is appropriate for investors seeking:
• Conservative fixed income exposure
• Current income generation
• Portfolio diversification
• Lower volatility compared to equity investments
• Investment horizon of 3+ years

The fund is suitable for moderate to conservative risk tolerance profiles and may serve as a core fixed income allocation within diversified portfolios. Associates should verify client investment objectives, time horizon, and risk tolerance align with the fund's conservative mandate before recommending.

TAX CONSIDERATIONS
Distributions are made monthly and typically consist of ordinary income. Approximately 15-25% of distributions may qualify for the municipal bond tax exemption when the fund holds qualifying securities. Consult tax advisors for individual circumstances.

For complete prospectus and additional information, contact Meridian Wealth Partners client services at 1-800-MWP-BOND. All performance data represents past performance and does not guarantee future results.
